evidence: `src/shared/components/PaneControlTab.tsx` centralizes device detection, event subscription, positioning strategy, safe-area math, tooltip orchestration, and button policy selection in one component, creating a broad mixed-responsibility ownership boundary.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` and `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` act as high-traffic global facades while also carrying deprecated compatibility aliases, which extends migration-era API surface into long-lived core entry points.

Overlay visibility contracts (`open`/`onOpenChange`) are threaded through multiple wrappers (`InstallInstructionsModal`, `DescriptionModal`, `MediaTypeFilter`, `InfoTooltip`, `ModalContainer`) with mostly pass-through behavior, indicating boundary flattening instead of localized ownership.

Task builders use very wide parameter bags with mixed concerns (routing intent, UI restoration, worker/runtime knobs) in `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, then re-expand/reshape again in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`.

Task param parsing is duplicated as many fallback chains in `src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts` (top-level vs `orchestrator_details` vs `full_orchestrator_payload` vs segment params), indicating abstraction layers are not canonical at boundaries.

Several hooks are thin wrappers over generic helpers with little local policy (`src/domains/generation/hooks/useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration.ts`, `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts`, `src/pages/Home/hooks/auth/useAuthReferralFinalize.ts`), increasing call-surface count without reducing complexity.

Generation-task lookup APIs in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` mix incompatible boundary behaviors: `resolveGenerationProjectId` throws for missing/scope errors, while `getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` encodes missing/scope as status values, and `getPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` throws again if mapping is absent.

Scoped mapping logic applies `.eq('project_id', options.projectId)` in query construction, then still checks `row.project_id !== options.projectId` afterward, making `scope_mismatch` effectively unreachable and collapsing distinct scope vs missing outcomes.

Error/result contracts are split across `src/shared/lib/operationResult.ts` and `supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts` with near-duplicate concepts but incompatible shapes/construction patterns, and consumers handle failures differently (`useLineageChain` converts to structured error while `clipManager/metadata` throws raw error).

Edge JSON parsing has ambiguous fail-open behavior in `supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts`: malformed/non-object JSON in loose mode becomes `{}` (same as empty body), reducing seam observability for downstream handlers.

Lightbox flow spreads state through large composition layers and very large dependency arrays (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/VideoLightbox.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx`), increasing local cognitive load.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts` combines reducer state, URL deep-linking, pair computation, frame updates, and navigation coordination in one hook, mixing responsibilities.
`src/shared/components/ProductTour/index.tsx` centralizes callback routing, step-state transitions, DOM event wiring, and navigation side effects in a single module with many behavioral branches.

src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx uses `React.Children.only(children) as React.ReactElement` when `asChild` is true, but props still allow broad `children` shapes via `React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof TooltipPrimitive.Trigger>`.

src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts performs optimistic cache writes by asserting cache payloads (`data as { items: GenerationRow[] }`, `data as Shot[]`, `previousData as GenerationRow[]`) after minimal guards; malformed cache entries can pass compile-time and fail at runtime.

src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts defines a union query action type, then re-casts each action payload back to specific query parameter types during execution, which bypasses compile-time validation of action/query shape compatibility.
src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx force-casts `generationsQuery.data` to `GenerationsPaginatedResponse | undefined`, masking upstream hook return-type drift.

findings.summary: Core shared facades mix stable ownership with migration compatibility aliases, blurring long-term module purpose.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports both canonical `reportRuntimeError` and deprecated `handleRuntimeError` alias from the same high-import module.
`client.ts` exports canonical runtime initializer and deprecated `initializeSupabaseClient` alias side by side in the main Supabase access facade.
findings.suggestion: Move deprecated aliases to explicit compatibility-only modules (or versioned adapters), keep core facade exports strictly canonical, and migrate callers via codemod plus enforced deprecation deadline.

findings.summary: Pane control UI has accumulated cross-cutting responsibilities that should be split into clearer architectural units.
evidence: `PaneControlTab.tsx` combines behavior policy (`getButtonsToShow`), event suppression, pointer handling, analytics/tour tags, tooltip concerns, pane positioning, safe-area adaptation, and desktop/mobile branching in one file.

The component imports multiple subsystem concerns (`mobile` hooks, pane config, positioning strategy, typed app events, tooltip primitives), making it an integration hub rather than a focused UI boundary.
findings.suggestion: Extract a pure button-policy module, a presentation component, and a thin orchestrator hook so device/event/layout logic is separated from rendering and easier to own per concern.

findings.summary: Overlay state ownership is spread across many wrapper components that mostly forward visibility contracts.
evidence: Multiple components accept `open`/`onOpenChange` (or optional equivalents) and pass them into lower-level primitives with minimal local policy.
`ModalContainer` already defines core dialog lifecycle handling, yet sibling wrappers continue to own/forward visibility state individually, creating repeated boundary decisions.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one overlay ownership pattern (container-owned or caller-owned), then provide focused adapters per overlay type to avoid repeated pass-through wrappers and reduce change ripple.

findings.summary: Task creation abstractions expose oversized mixed-concern parameter objects that are repeatedly reshaped across layers.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskParams` and `IndividualTravelSegmentParams` combine transport fields, UI restoration fields, orchestration linkage, and runtime worker toggles in single interfaces.
Payload builders then selectively spread/omit these fields into new payload objects, while consumers later recover values via multi-location fallback parsing.
The same concepts (prompt/model/resolution/orchestrator linkage) exist in multiple shapes across modules instead of one canonical contract.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a versioned canonical task contract per task family (creator input, persisted payload, viewer model) with explicit mappers between stages, and deprecate ad-hoc optional passthrough fields.

findings.summary: Multiple hooks/facades add indirection without enforcing meaningful boundary policy.
evidence: `useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` mainly forwards normalized input to repository call plus generic error reporting.
`useDiscordSignIn` and `useAuthReferralFinalize` largely wrap `runAuthSupabaseOperation` and storage reads/writes with minimal additional domain behavior.
`getSupabaseClient` is a pass-through facade to runtime client and is then wrapped again at hook level.
findings.suggestion: Collapse single-purpose pass-through wrappers into fewer domain-level operations, keeping wrappers only where they add validation, policy, or lifecycle coordination.

findings.summary: Orchestration handoff relies on fallback query and routing paths instead of one deterministic inter-module contract.
evidence: Completion logic in `orchestrator.ts` queries siblings by run-id first, then falls back to orchestrator reference filter when missing.
Generation routing in `generation.ts` checks several overlapping fields (`based_on`, `child_generation_id`, `parent_generation_id`, stitch task types, flags) to decide behavior.
Creator-side payload composition emits multiple optional routing fields and mode-specific branches, increasing chances of contract drift.
findings.suggestion: Define one required orchestration identity + one required generation-routing enum, enforce it at task creation, and make completion/generation handlers reject ambiguous payloads early.

findings.summary: Lightbox internals are over-composed into large prop/model objects with heavy dependency churn, reducing local clarity.
evidence: `VideoLightboxProps` plus grouped sub-props create broad object surfaces and many conditional rendering branches.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` builds several memoized composite objects with long dependency lists, making dataflow hard to trace and easy to destabilize.
`LightboxLayout` consumes many context slices and orchestrates multiple overlay/control concerns in one layout component.
findings.suggestion: Split lightbox into stricter feature slices (navigation, variants, editing, overlays) with narrow typed contracts and colocate render decisions with each slice to reduce global model construction.

findings.summary: Generation task repository exposes mixed throw-vs-status error semantics across adjacent APIs, making handoffs unpredictable.
evidence: `resolveGenerationProjectId` throws on not found/project mismatch/missing scope, while `getPrimaryTaskMappingsForGenerations` returns status-coded records and null task IDs.
`getPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` throws if no mapping exists even though mapping API already models absence with `missing_generation`.
findings.suggestion: Standardize repository outputs on one contract (prefer a typed result object with explicit status/error code), and keep throwing only at a single adapter boundary so cache/hook consumers can compose behavior consistently.

findings.summary: Project-scoped mapping can misclassify scope mismatches as missing generations due to contradictory filtering.
evidence: Query adds `.eq('project_id', options.projectId)` before fetch, so rows with different project IDs are excluded up front.
Later branch still checks `row.project_id !== options.projectId` to emit `scope_mismatch`, but those rows generally cannot appear, so mismatches are reported as `missing_generation`.
findings.suggestion: Either remove server-side project filter and classify in-process, or keep filter and remove `scope_mismatch` from this API; if scope diagnostics are required, add a dedicated scoped-verification query path.

findings.summary: Client and edge layers maintain parallel failure/success wrappers with incompatible shapes, increasing translation friction at integration seams.
evidence: `operationResult` includes `toOperationResultError` and options-based constructors; `edgeOperation` uses different constructor signatures and no equivalent error-conversion helper.
Consumers diverge: `src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts` converts failures to structured errors, while `src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipManager/metadata.ts` throws `result.error` directly.
findings.suggestion: Define a shared result envelope spec (field names, error metadata, conversion rules) and align both modules plus callers to it, or centralize one cross-runtime helper package.

findings.summary: Loose JSON parsing collapses malformed payloads and empty bodies into the same `{}` result, obscuring boundary intent.
evidence: `parseJsonBodyLoose` returns `{}` for invalid JSON and non-object JSON via fail-open paths.
Downstream operations rely on parsed request data for storage behavior; without a parse-origin signal, handlers cannot distinguish omitted inputs from malformed client payloads.
findings.suggestion: Return a typed parse outcome (`body`, `parseStatus`, optional `parseErrorCode`) even in loose mode so handlers can decide whether to degrade, reject, or audit malformed payloads.

findings.summary: Deprecated alias exports preserve duplicate call paths and migration noise.
evidence: `initializeSupabaseClient` is a deprecated alias of `initializeSupabaseClientRuntime` in the same module.
`handleRuntimeError` is a deprecated alias of `reportRuntimeError` in the same module.
Both modules therefore maintain two public names for one behavior, which prolongs migration debt and weakens local clarity.
findings.suggestion: Execute a bounded migration: update all internal imports to the canonical names (`initializeSupabaseClientRuntime`, `reportRuntimeError`), add temporary lint/import restrictions to block new alias usage, then remove the deprecated alias exports in one cleanup PR.

findings.summary: Tool-specific edit workflow hooks are organized under `shared/hooks`, blurring ownership and change boundaries.
evidence: `useEditToolMediaPersistence` persists and restores editor-specific media state, queries `generations`, and depends on tool settings semantics rather than generic hook concerns.
`useEditVideoSettings` hardcodes `TOOL_IDS.EDIT_VIDEO` and edit-video defaults, making it feature-owned logic in a shared namespace.
findings.suggestion: Move these hooks and their tests into a tool-owned path (for example `src/tools/edit-video/hooks/*`) and leave only truly reusable primitives in `src/shared/hooks`.

findings.summary: Edit-video defaults are stored in `shared/lib`, creating package-level ambiguity between shared primitives and tool-owned configuration.
evidence: `editVideoDefaults.ts` defines settings tied to `TOOL_IDS.EDIT_VIDEO` and video-editing fields (`selectedVideoGenerationId`, `portionStartTime`, `loras`).
`useEditVideoSettings.ts` consumes this file directly as feature configuration, showing the module is not broadly shared infrastructure.
findings.suggestion: Relocate `editVideoDefaults.ts` to an edit-video feature/tool package and expose only a minimal shared contract if other tools truly depend on it.

findings.summary: Contract governance is declared in docs but not operationalized into enforceable quality gates.
evidence: README.md states shared-entrypoint exports are stable and prescribes a migration sequence.
The facade files rely on comments/tags (@publicContract, @deprecated) only; no linked enforcement mechanism is present in these contracts.
This leaves compatibility policy as convention rather than an architectural control.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a contract registry plus CI check (or lint rule) requiring explicit contract annotations, deprecation metadata, and migration status for all declared stable-entrypoint modules.

findings.summary: Compatibility aliases exist without sunset criteria, encouraging permanent dual surfaces.
evidence: handleRuntimeError is kept as a deprecated alias with no target removal milestone.
initializeSupabaseClient is similarly preserved as a deprecated shim without retirement trigger.
README.md mandates staged migration but does not require lifecycle metadata (version/date/owner) for deprecations.
findings.suggestion: Require deprecations to include owner and removal target (date or release), and fail CI when deprecated exports exceed their sunset window.

findings.summary: The contract taxonomy mixes runtime facades and UI primitives under one stability policy, blurring ownership boundaries.
evidence: README.md places high-fan-in runtime facades and UI primitives in the same stable-contract bucket.
cn.ts and button.tsx expose public utilities/components but contain no explicit contract intent markers unlike runtime facade files.
This makes it unclear whether UI API changes follow the same compatibility obligations as runtime integrations.
findings.suggestion: Split governance into explicit contract classes (runtime integration contracts vs UI primitive contracts) with separate change policies and required in-file markers.

findings.summary: `TooltipTrigger` exposes `asChild` without a matching single-element child type contract, then force-casts at render time.
evidence: In `tooltip.tsx`, `TooltipTrigger` accepts broad trigger props and uses `React.Children.only(children) as React.ReactElement` for `asChild`.
In `PaneControlTab.tsx`, `TooltipButton` forwards `children: React.ReactNode` into `<TooltipTrigger asChild>`, so compile-time does not prevent non-single-element usage.
findings.suggestion: Use a discriminated prop type for `TooltipTrigger` (e.g., `asChild: true` requires `children: React.ReactElement`) and remove the `as React.ReactElement` cast.

findings.summary: Optimistic mutation logic relies on unchecked cache shape assertions that can desynchronize static types from runtime payloads.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations.ts` updates cache via `(data as { items: GenerationRow[] }).items.map(...)` after only checking `'items' in data`, without validating array element shape.
The same file also asserts `(data as Shot[])` and `(previousData as GenerationRow[])` before mapping, so non-matching query payloads become runtime failures despite passing TS.
findings.suggestion: Replace assertions with typed query keys and runtime guards (`Array.isArray`, per-item shape checks), or centralize cache update helpers with generic constraints per key.

findings.summary: Query lifecycle actions are typed as a union but executed through branch re-casts, weakening mode-to-query compatibility checks.
evidence: `useGenerationInvalidation.ts` defines `QueryLifecycleAction` with `mode` plus a union query payload, then calls `refetchQueries`/`invalidateQueries` with `as Parameters<...>[0]` casts.
This pattern allows construction of structurally invalid action/query pairs without compiler rejection at the call-site where actions are built.
findings.suggestion: Use a discriminated union with mode-specific query payload types and remove downstream casts in `executeQueryLifecycleActions`.

findings.summary: Join Clips page force-casts query data instead of consuming a strongly typed query result contract.
evidence: `JoinClipsPage.tsx` sets `const videosData = generationsQuery.data as GenerationsPaginatedResponse | undefined;`.
The assertion bypasses inference from `useProjectGenerations`, so return-type drift would compile but break downstream access (`videosData?.items`).
findings.suggestion: Parameterize `useProjectGenerations` with its concrete response type (or wrap it in a typed selector hook) and remove the page-level cast.
